html:
Forward camera translation: each red arrow lies on the optical axis.
⚠ Figure failed to load

elements: Five identical cameras represented by frustums with image planes
Five thick red arrows indicating forward motion direction aligned with the camera's optical axis
Five black points marking the focal centers of the cameras

interactions.label: Camera Spacing
interactions.teaches: Shows how varying the spacing between camera positions affects the visual representation of forward translation through the scene.

interactions.label: Arrow Length
interactions.teaches: Demonstrates the magnitude of forward velocity by varying the length of the arrows indicating camera movement.

interactions.label: Number of Cameras
interactions.teaches: Adds or removes camera positions to illustrate the path and progression of the camera moving forward through the scene.

demo_steps.title: Single Forward Move
demo_steps.narration: Let's begin with two cameras and a short motion. Notice how the red arrow points straight along the camera's axis, representing forward movement. This illustrates the simplest case of forward translation.

demo_steps.title: Visualizing Path Progression
demo_steps.narration: Now, let's add more camera positions. As the number of cameras increases, you can see a sequence of forward motions—each camera advances along the same line, showing the progression as the camera moves deeper into the scene.

demo_steps.title: Changing Speed and Spacing
demo_steps.narration: Finally, let's increase both the spacing between cameras and the length of the arrows. The longer arrows now show a faster forward motion, and the wider spacing illustrates larger steps between camera locations—emphasizing the physical meaning of camera translation speed.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Position the virtual camera orthogonally to the row of camera frustums, at a distance where all are visible. Start with a moderate zoom so the entire sequence of cameras and arrows fits comfortably in view.
interactionPlan.notes: Render thick red arrows for clarity. Maintain identical camera frustum geometry for each position. Ensure camera image planes remain perpendicular to the direction of motion. Use basic lambertian shading, and keep background simple and uncluttered.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The spacing between camera frustum elements is tighter in the generated figure than in the source.
Arrowheads in the generated figure are less visually prominent compared to the bold, 2D style in the source.
The camera frustum geometry is more 3D and detailed in the generated version, while the source depicts them with a stylized 2D black outline.
There is some unintended transparency/3D surface shading on the generated camera image planes not present in the source.
The thickness and color saturation of the arrows (especially the shaft) is less vivid than the saturated red in the source.
evaluation.notes: Excellent interactive and pedagogical controls, but minor visual style and spacing mismatches reduce faithfulness to the original 2D schematic.
action_items: Increase the thickness and color saturation of the red arrows to better match the bold 2D style of the source figure.
Adjust spacing between cameras to more closely resemble the original figure's consistent spacing and proportion.
Consider simplifying the image plane (remove extra 3D surface shading/opacity) for a purer outline aesthetic.

feedback.rationale: All issues noted involve visual styling, spacing, and rendering details rather than conceptual or planning errors.
action_items: Increase the thickness and color saturation of the red arrows to better match the bold 2D style of the source figure.
Adjust spacing between cameras to more closely resemble the original figure's consistent spacing and proportion.
Consider simplifying the image plane (remove extra 3D surface shading/opacity) for a purer outline aesthetic.

attempts.html: ⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: Arrowheads in the generated figure are darker red rather than bright red.
Arrow shafts are slightly narrower and shorter compared to original image proportion.
Camera frustums are missing the subtle inner shading seen in the 2D reference.
Black dots (focal points) are somewhat smaller than in the reference.
No image plane transparency effect, making the 3D version look visually denser.
evaluation.notes: Figure captures all major geometric elements and the interactive controls are meaningful and well-labeled, though visual fidelity could be improved in arrow coloring and camera frustum shading.
action_items: Brighten the red color of arrows to more closely match the original reference.
Add subtle transparency or shading inside the camera frustums to mimic the 2D illustration's look.

feedback.rationale: All issues are related to visual fidelity and implementation details rather than conceptual understanding or planning.
action_items: Brighten the red color of arrows to more closely match the original reference.
Add subtle transparency or shading inside the camera frustums to mimic the 2D illustration's look.

attempts.html:
Forward camera translation: each red arrow lies on the optical axis.
⚠ Figure failed to load

discrepancies: The camera frustum shapes are filled with gray and rendered as 3D solid cones, versus the original wireframe pentagon or quadrilateral prism style.
The red arrows are slightly thinner and less visually prominent than those in the original which are flatter and bolder.
Camera frustums in the generated figure are not as sharply outlined in black as in the 2D source; edges are less pronounced.
The spacing between cameras and arrows can be adjusted but does not exactly match the fixed proportion and layout in the original figure.
There is additional UI and a gray line in the image plane absent from the source.
evaluation.notes: Strong interactive pedagogical tool; 3D interpretation closely matches but with mild stylistic differences in frustum renderings and arrow style.
action_items: Refine the camera frustum geometry to appear more wireframe and match the sharp black polygonal outline of the original 2D figure.
Increase the boldness and flatness of the red arrows so they more closely replicate the original visual impact.
Reduce the prominence of additional elements like the gray center line and soften 3D shading to appear more like the plain linework style of the textbook image.
Offer a 'static mode' or 'match textbook view' preset to align spacing/appearance directly with the reference image.
Consider an extra toggle to hide the UI/narration for snapshot matching the original exact look.

feedback.rationale: The conceptual plan is correct, but implementation details such as frustum wireframe rendering, arrow styling, layout spacing, and presence of extraneous UI require refinement.
